programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in Milton Keynes is 52-year-old hypnotherapist Howard, whos hoping his fancy menu will be a hit.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day two in Milton Keynes, and the turn of 30-year-old outspoken host Louise. She is hoping an evening of fabulous food will win her the prize, but once her guests see her menu they arent impressed.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the third day of the competition in Milton Keynes and the turn of management consultant David. David has a real treat for his guests tonight, with dancing Vegas show girls!

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the fourth day of the competition in Milton Keynes and Helens turn to host. Shes hoping a night of `East meets West will infuse her guests with the culinary excitement needed after a few nights of mediocre fare.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final day of the competition in Milton Keynes its the turn of competitive salesman Simon, who feels his night of fine dining will truly impress this critical bunch.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host in Newcastle is workaholic and mum-of-three Clare. Clare plans to get the week off to a hot and spicy start with her Indian themed menu of spicy pakoras and lamb curry.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Comedy promoter David hopes to impress with his extravagant menu full of locally sourced produce. Theres caviar, scallops, beef fillet and oysters, but will his luxurious food be enough to win over his guests?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the third night of the competition in Newcastle and the turn of sophisticated marketing consultant Liz to impress her guests with a French themed menu, but is Liz really as posh as her guests first thought?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the fourth night in Newcastle and club singer John plans to bag the prize with a menu inspired by his travels around the world. If the grubs not to everyones taste, Johns got a musical surprise up his sleeve, but he faces a tough challenge.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the last night of the competition in Newcastle and the turn of hotel administrator Vicky to host. As well as taking on some hefty competition, Vicky also has to try to keep the peace when John lets David know exactly what he thinks of him.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In north London and Hertfordshire, Dawn and Simon bond with hipsters Darren and Sian, but can any of them find common ground with multi-lingual lawyer Ayesha and astrophysicist Stephen?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: We revisit Shane, who dreamt of a new life on the north Kent coast, and Indu and Jason, who wanted a family home with potential in Guildford. Did they get what they wished for?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Gill and Rhys from Leicestershire love the charming Dordogne countryside so much that a permanent move is on the cards. Craig Rowe shows them what their budget of £190,000 can buy in Bergerac.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton flies to Gran Canaria to help BandB owners Andy and Julian find their dream property. They have a budget of £125,000 and Laura has five great properties to show them.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Teacher Wendys looking for her dream holiday home in Puglia, southern Italy. Jean Johannson lines up five great properties for her £200k, but will any of them get top marks?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Help! I Bought It at Auction with Sarah Beeny
programme.desc: Christy and Matt pour £1m into a seven-bed house in serious need of TLC. Heather and Dave take on converted stables that they bought when they were on holiday!

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Theres one last hooray in the Sunday School hall before it becomes the pottery studio, as Keith and Marj turn it into a party venue and bring singing back to the Chapel.

programme.title: Salisbury Poisonings: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The shocking story of the 2018 poisoning of ex-Russian spy Sergei Skripal, his daughter Yulia and Salisbury local Dawn Sturgess. Told through intimate first-hand testimony and with unparalleled access to police, doctors, scientists and the military.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Dad-to-be Josh, whos 27, has come off his motorbike at speed. Georgie brings in her 10-month-old son Phoenix, whos been sucking on a bleach tablet.

programme.title: The Deadly Companions
programme.desc: Soon after the Civil War, a Yankee officer arrives in town and saves a card cheat from a lynching. Their intention of robbing the poorly guarded bank is thwarted when masked raiders beat them to it and in an ensuing gun-fight, the young son of a cabaret dancer is accidentally shot dead. The remorseful officer insists on escorting the dancer with the body when she decides to bury her son in Apache territory.

programme.title: Sgeulachd Cogaidh na h-Artaig
programme.desc: Discover why the desire to capture Nazi weapon technology turns the U.S. and USSR from WW II allies into Cold War enemies; look at how the superpowers try to gain an Arctic foothold, from guerrilla warfare to intelligence schemes to nuclear arms.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBCs Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Vivek Singh and Donna Hay, and by special guest Lucy Beaumont. Drinks expert Olly Smith picks the wines to go with the studio dishes, and there are also some great moments from the BBC food archive.

programme.title: Chris Cooks Cymru
programme.desc: Chris explores Narberths indie food scene, from Spanish tapas and wild Welsh vermouth to Dexter beef, foraging and a fire-cooked feast celebrating Pembrokeshire creativity.

programme.desc: Featuring tournament news, player interviews, match analyses and other titbits from the world of English and Scottish football.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A marathon runner completes a marathon seven-year project to transform a site in Llanfynydd into smart holiday accommodation, while a couple give a terraced house in Derby the wow factor and friends in Southend-on-Sea create a lovely coastal home.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents Bargain Hunt from Oswestry in Shropshire. With the help of experts Charles Hanson and Ben Cooper, two teams compete to buy the most profitable items to sell at auction.

programme.desc: Coverage of day 15 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Michael McIntyres Big Show

programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of five Premier League games as title-chasing Arsenal take on Fulham at the Emirates. Relegation-threatened West Ham are in action at Brentford, and Brighton continue their push for European football next season at Newcastle. Elsewhere, Sunderland travels to Wolves, and theres action from the Friday night game between Leeds and Burnley.

programme.title: The Football Interview
programme.desc: Kelly Somers in conversation with Elliot Anderson.

programme.title: Point Break
programme.desc: An undercover FBI agent in California pursues a gang of bank robbers known as the `Ex-Presidents, who lead him to the LA surfing scene and an adrenaline junkie who lives for thrills. The cop soon finds that he is being seduced by the fast-paced lifestyle of his new mentor.

programme.title: Scotlands Home of the Year
programme.desc: Interior designers Anna Campbell Jones and Banjo Beale and architect Danny Campbell travel the country in search of Scotlands best homes. In the west, the judges must choose between a Georgian town house in Ayr, a semi-detached house in Dumbreck and a Victorian villa in Kilmacolm. Scoring them on architectural merit, distinctive design and personal style, which home will Anna, Banjo and Danny judge worthy enough to go forward to the grand final?

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: Will and Dan get a model of the worlds only rotating boat lift back up and running, and Dom collects a precious toy that saw its owner through pioneering pre-natal treatment.

programme.title: Schemers

programme.desc: From growing up gay in the 80s and 90s, to working on building sites and becoming a successful stand-up comic, Susie McCabes life is jam-packed with rich material.

programme.title: The Singer Story: Made in Clydebank
programme.desc: The Singer sewing machine, international icon and a symbol of the Industrial Revolution, helped to put the town of Clydebank on the map. Over the course of a century, it employed tens of thousands of `bankies before its demise in 1980.

programme.title: Lockerbie: Our Story
programme.desc: Decades after the UKs worst terrorist attack, six families and friends tell the story of their lost loved ones for the first time.

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: Twilight Monthly magazine needs a new cover star and Victor is convinced he is the man for the job. Meanwhile, Winston battles with a neighbour to win a stray cats affections.

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Chief Commissioner Miekelson tries out some weapons, gets his portrait painted and does something about bigotry. Meanwhile, the rural police deal with a cat stranded in a boat and the urban police are not fooled by a bluffing burglar. Volunteer Officer Ken Beattie fines a litterbug, Operator Maggie LeBeau discusses her heroes, and Sergeant Karen Ann Millar is offered fingerprints and some glockenspiel music from local man Bobby Muir.

programme.desc: Gary Robertson brings you all the main issues from the world of politics and beyond. Big interviews, discussion and analysis of the stories that matter to Scotland.

programme.title: Loop
programme.desc: Moy Mackay draws on the surroundings of the Scottish Borders that she calls home to make her felt artwork. Moy uses traditional crafting techniques combined with a love of painting to create her artworks from merino fleece.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Four very different schoolgirls are thrown together in detention. As they plan their escape, the situation spirals out of control.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Clara is alone in Victorian London with a man she doesnt know, a dinosaur in the Thames, and a spate of deadly spontaneous combustions. The Doctor has changed. Its time you knew him.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: When a historical documentary team comes to film at Button House, Alison and the ghosts are shocked to discover that the assassination plot they are investigating involved one of their own. Meanwhile, Mike, always on the lookout for free publicity, begs Alison to accept the offer of a television interview about the old house. The only hitch is that Alison is terrified of speaking on camera. Luckily, Thomas is there to coach her in the art of speechmaking.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor arrives at Button House with a revelation that leaves Alison reeling. Thomas is equally confused by his feelings for this strange visitor, while Lady Button and Julian warn of sinister motives, and Kitty is as territorial as ever over her surrogate sister Alison. Mike has an epiphany on his first day at a new job, which triggers Julian to remember his first day as a ghost.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Mirandas mother Penny is constantly disappointed that Miranda runs a joke shop rather than having a proper job. Penny bumps into one of Mirandas old school friends, Tilly, and lies that Miranda has a great new job, so Miranda decides to prove everyone wrong and find a new career. After getting caught by Tilly when helping out as a waitress, she finally decides to come clean and be proud of her real job.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda knows she is not exactly cool, but being young, free and single has its advantages, like jetting off to Thailand on holiday for a few days. Although, when it comes down to it, she would rather not bother with the hassle of going quite so far, so she books herself into a hotel across the road instead.

programme.title: Quiz Lady
programme.desc: Anne and her estranged train-wreck of a sister, Jenny, must work together to help cover their mothers gambling debts. When Annes beloved dog is kidnapped, they set out on a wild cross-country trek to get the cash.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny makes a terrible discovery about Corinthians parentage, and Janet and Donna both go for the job of manager of the Archer.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet starts her job as manager of The Archer and gives the pub a World War II theme to appeal to the grey pound, Donna is intent on revenge, Jonny tries to help Gaz become a Dad, and Louise becomes a prostitute.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet tries desperately to find a way of keeping the pub open, Louise is feeling unwell and Kelly thinks she recognises the symptoms, Jonny thinks he has fallen in love with Gaz, and Donnas little brother causes chaos when he comes to stay.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: The Archer is about to be demolished in this special horror episode. The gang decide to break in for one last night together, but once inside, strange things start to happen and they find themselves trapped inside a pub that seems intent on killing them, one by one.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet, Donna and Louise compete to convince the pubs new manager that they have the best personality to be the new barmaid. Jonny wins a competition to go shark-jumping in Hawaii.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: With Jonny dead, Janet decides she must get through the five stages of grief - DABDA - quicker than Darren Day on a stage full of blondes. Gaz finds a painful way to help her.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Mirandas mother Penny is constantly disappointed that Miranda runs a joke shop rather than having a proper job. Penny bumps into one of Mirandas old school friends, Tilly, and lies that Miranda has a great new job, so Miranda decides to prove everyone wrong and find a new career. After getting caught by Tilly when helping out as a waitress, she finally decides to come clean and be proud of her real job.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda knows she is not exactly cool, but being young, free and single has its advantages, like jetting off to Thailand on holiday for a few days. Although, when it comes down to it, she would rather not bother with the hassle of going quite so far, so she books herself into a hotel across the road instead.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max attempts to court Cindy, Walford welcomes a surprise visitor, and the Gulatis and the Panesars try to support Ravi.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Mitchells try to get through to Phil, Cindy and Max have a close call, and the Slaters surprise Big Mo.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phils search continues, two Walford residents face a race against time, and Nigel receives some visitors.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Albert Square comes together to celebrate a friend, Phil battles his inner demons, and Max and Cindys romance causes friction.

programme.title: Leigh-Anne: Race, Pop and Power
programme.desc: This powerful documentary follows pop star Leigh-Anne Pinnock as she confronts her experience as the only black member of Little Mix, and as a black woman in the music industry. Leigh-Anne talks about the racism she experienced growing up. Both her parents are of Caribbean heritage, and Leigh-Anne identifies as black. She is also aware that having lighter skin and being a celebrity means she is in a more privileged position than others.

programme.title: The Woman in Black
programme.desc: A widowed lawyer encounters ghostly goings-on at the foreboding estate of a recently deceased client.

programme.title: Paranormal: Britains Last Witch
programme.desc: Tian Eleri investigates the story of Scottish medium Helen Duncan, who in 1944 became the last person convicted and imprisoned under the Witchcraft Act. As the Blitz rained down on London, Helen went on trail at the Old Bailey.

programme.title: Paranormal: Britains Last Witch
programme.desc: Theres an unnerving breakthrough in Sians investigation into a woman who claimed to summon spirits of the dead when she finds an audio recording of a Helen Duncan seance. Not only is a gravelly male spirit heard to speak, but the people gathered comment that they see and recognise him. How is this possible? Sian tests out different theories - was it someone else speaking?

programme.title: Paranormal: Britains Last Witch
programme.sub-title: Britains Last Witch: Bringing Back the Dead
programme.desc: Theres only a handful of mediums worldwide who claim to do anything close to what Helen Duncan said she could and physically manifest spirits into a room. For Sian, the only way to fully understand what was happening at Helens seances is to witness this for herself, so she tracks one of these rare mediums down in the Netherlands. Sian arrives at the Dutch monastery and is greeted by a host who takes Sian on a private tour of the seance room, where the medium will work that evening.

programme.title: Paranormal: Britains Last Witch
programme.sub-title: Britains Last Witch: The Final Seance
programme.desc: Sians mission to get to the truth behind Helen Duncans apparently supernatural abilities draws to a close - but a secret package sent from Helens granddaughter in America leaves Sian with what appears to be the smoking gun she has been waiting for. The package contains what seems to be unexplainable proof that a spirit revealed the sinking of a second warship during one of Helens seances.

programme.title: Leigh-Anne: Race, Pop and Power
programme.desc: This powerful documentary follows pop star Leigh-Anne Pinnock as she confronts her experience as the only black member of Little Mix, and as a black woman in the music industry. Leigh-Anne talks about the racism she experienced growing up. Both her parents are of Caribbean heritage, and Leigh-Anne identifies as black. She is also aware that having lighter skin and being a celebrity means she is in a more privileged position than others.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Clara is alone in Victorian London with a man she doesnt know, a dinosaur in the Thames, and a spate of deadly spontaneous combustions. The Doctor has changed. Its time you knew him.

programme.desc: Coverage of day 15 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: The Top of the Pops vaults are opened up to relive some of the classic and biggest songs of 1992. Featuring the hip hop and dance of Kris Kross, SL2, Bizarre Inc and Felix to the rock stylings of Manic Street Preachers and Primal Scream.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A look back at some of the songs performed on the show in 1993. Dance music and Euro pop continued to dominate the charts with performances from Haddaway, Sub Sub, Ace of Base and Robin S. The early stages of Britpop began to emerge with Suede and Radiohead plus Icelandic superstar Bjork makes an early appearance. Then there are the big sellers from Meat Loaf and Gabrielle and the grooves came courtesy of Jamiroquai and Chaka Demus and Pliers plus many more.

programme.title: Black Box
programme.desc: Obsessive forensic analyst Mathieu Vasseur is disappointed to be sidelined in the examination of the black box of an Atrian 800 airliner which has mysteriously crashed in the Alps. When his boss goes missing, Mathieu is asked to step in, and he concludes that there was an unauthorised entry into the cockpit. A press conference is held to announce the findings, but Mathieu is subsequently presented with conflicting evidence.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon, the daughter of a crime family searches for romance and falls for Arron, a member of a rival crime family. They develop a life-changing bond as things begin to implode for Shannons family.

programme.title: Mint

programme.title: Here We Are
programme.desc: Aharon has devoted his life to raising his son Uri. They live together in a gentle routine, away from the real world. But Uri is autistic, and now as a young adult it might be time for him to live in a specialized home. While on their way to the institution, Aharon decides to run away with his son and hits the road, knowing that Uri is not ready for this separation. Or is it, in fact, his father who is not ready?

programme.desc: Series following life in the Gold familys huge and colourful pawn shop, where every day they encounter great treasures, bizarre collectibles, old junk and crooked sellers.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Series following life in the Gold familys pawn shop. A threatening customer puts the whole store on high alert. Ashley and Seth bet on whether a stripper pole will sell.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his dealers are at Ludlow in Shropshire today, where Corrie Jeffery has a laugh with a seller who brings in an eastern style vase, David Hakeney takes a gamble on a vintage horse racing game and one seller nets a large sum for her charm bracelet.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna
programme.desc: For nearly 400 years, Gloucester Massachusetts has been home to Americas oldest seaport and the men who depend on fishing for survival. Each summer brings the chance to make a fortune with the oceans most lucrative prize giant blue-fin tuna.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna
programme.desc: The paydays are bigger than ever as the rival fleets head out on the first day of another perilous bluefin tuna fishing season.

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: Troy Landry and Pickle Wheat get a tip about a place called the Snake Pit from a local; Bill and Tes investigate a rock quarry fully of Pythons; Dusty has a close call with the supernatural, while Zak and Aaron fight to protect the local children.

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: The battle for the Everglades becomes personal for Troy and Pickle. Bill and Tes venture further north than ever before. Zak and Aaron visit Zaks childhood stomping grounds, while Dusty heads to one of his favourite honey holes.

programme.title: Gold Wars Downunder
programme.desc: Gold Coast socialite couple, Kimberley and Ritchie, make the tough decision to move from the Gold Coast to the mine to help Dan out. (S1, ep 1)

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Across the globe, reports of mysterious glowing orbs are on the rise; their movements frequently defy the laws of physics, and now they are even being reported near military bases, ships, and nuclear weapon facilities.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Worm in the Sky, Skeletal Mystery Remains, and Thessaloniki Mystery Sounds
programme.desc: A look at what the odd, worm-like object seen in the sky in a video Tweeted by a Proof viewer is.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Spontaneous Human Combustion, the Tell-Tale Hum, and UFO Explosion
programme.desc: Are the photos taken in 1951 proof that a woman in St. Petersburg, Florida spontaneously combusted? What is the brain-numbing noise plaguing Brooklyn residents? Tony Harris and his team of experts delve into these claims to uncover the truth.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Teen UFO, Flying Dinosaur Over Idaho, and Headless Goats
programme.desc: Tony and his team investigate a series of anomalies, including alleged UFO footage from Sweden, a purported pterodactyl sighting in Idaho and a bizarre case of decapitated goats in Atlanta.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: NASA astronaut Mike Barratt explains photographing a strange ring in the ice on the worlds oldest and most mysterious lake from the International Space Station; more rings on Lake Baikal have been seen.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A possible smoke screen over North Korea concerns military analysts; an amateur archaeologist makes a fantastic discovery of not one but two forgotten pyramids in Egypt.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Images of earth from space show an intimate portrait of human activity; cities glow bright while the wilderness remains dark, but the Australian outback is lit up like a series of vast cities.

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows
programme.desc: What started as a Bavarian group of revolutionaries intent on infiltrating powerful institutions has now become the stuff of legend. Some claim this elite group continues to control power from behind closed doors.

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing is teaching Charlie how to throw, but when he throws the ball in the lounge it knocks the fruit over! Bing starts to explain, but Charlie interrupts, leaving Flop to believe Charlie did it. Bing happily shows Charlie how to voosh Squeaky instead, but it flies out of his hands and hits Flops tray of snacks! After some encouragement, Bing tells Flop the truth and apologises.

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are finding out about an incredible fishy adventure called the salmon run. Every year, salmon swim from the oceans where they live all the way upstream to calm, shallow rivers to lay their eggs. On the way they have to leap up waterfalls, jump fish ladders and dash past hungry bears who try to catch them. Kyan thinks these fish are even better at fantastic gymnastics than he is! Ubercorn sets those co-ordinates for the Kenai River, Alaska.

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: Hey Duggee

Hiding behind his dad, Roly wasnt loud at all but impossibly quiet. As Duggee shows Roly around the Clubhouse, he squeals, hides and hangs off his dad, and just seems altogether too shy to fit in. Duggee has a great idea to introduce Roly to Norrie, thinking a friend will help him to settle in.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Next, the Squirrels watch the bugs on the tree and put on a play to catch the differences. The Squirrels watch the play, but when they watch it again, the bugs are different. They are now a banana and a clown. The Squirrels see it right away as they are getting better. Now its time to play ultimate spot the difference. The Squirrels go outside the Clubhouse with Duggee and watch all the animals take formation. We see a complete match on the right and left sides.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Sawcliffe Manor in High Risby, Lincolnshire, where hosts Emily and Jana are convinced their stylish decor and rural setting will give them the edge. But their chances of hosting a flawless visit last all of two seconds.

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: A definitive one-stop shop for all things cruise, bringing you the hottest destinations and the latest deals to ensure that your next cruise ship holiday is your greatest one yet.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler doesnt want new flatmate Janine to make the apartment too `girly, Ross overdoses on tooth whitener before a date, and Phoebe tells Rachel that she kissed Ralph Lauren at Rachels workplace.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Got High
programme.desc: On Thanksgiving, Chandler tries to gain favour with Monicas parents Jack and Judy, but an old secret involving Ross causes problems that he is unaware of. Meanwhile, Rachel proves she is no domestic goddess when she creates a quite disastrous dessert.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey plots the perfect opportunity to kiss Janine, while Ross and Monica resurrect their silly high school dance routine for a television special.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Apothecary Table
programme.desc: Joey is on cloud nine when he starts to date Janine, but she doesnt like his friends, and Rachel worries that Phoebe wont appreciate the way she furnishes their apartment.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross and Chandler bicker over which of them created a raunchy joke, Phoebe antagonises Monica and Rachel with her candour, and Joey reluctantly takes a job at Central Perk.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey gets in trouble for giving female customers free food at Central Perk, and after her father cuts her off, Rachels sister Jill comes to town. Rachel talks Jill into asking Ross out, and later, Ross and Jill return to his apartment. As he closes the drapes, Rachel looks on from Monica and Chandlers apartment.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Cant Cry
programme.desc: Chandler admits that he hasnt cried since he was a child, Phoebe discovers that Ursula is starring in porno movies using her name, and Rachel desperately tries to stop her sister Jill from starting a relationship with Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One That Could Have Been
programme.desc: Rachel tells her friends about Barry and Mindys impending divorce and wonders what life would have been like if she had married him. This leads the others to speculate how their lives could have turned out too.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One That Could Have Been
programme.desc: In the second episode showing what could have happened to the friends had their lives taken different paths, Carol and Ross decide to have a threesome to spice up their marriage, while Rachel goes to Joeys and kisses him and then catches Barry cheating.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler gets into trouble with his homemade gift for Monica, Ross tries to teach martial arts to Rachel and Phoebe, and Joey hires a look-alike.

programme.title: Bring It On
programme.desc: A young girl finds that being made captain of her high schools award-winning cheerleading team isnt all plain sailing. Her parents want her to study more, her guy wants to see her more and her predecessor stole routines from a tough inner-city school, whose captain aims to get even.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Dates a Student
programme.desc: When a fire damages their apartment, Rachel and Phoebe must choose between living temporarily with Joey or Monica. Meanwhile, Ross gets into trouble at work, and Chandler tries to help Joeys career.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross worries when his student girlfriend Liz goes on holiday with her wild friends. Meanwhile, Rachel is desperate to find a date for a charity ball, and Joeys fridge breaks down.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Mac and C.H.E.E.S.E.
programme.desc: Joey is excited about his audition for a major television action series about a man and his crimefighting robot, but Chandler almost ruins everything.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Meets Elizabeths Dad
programme.desc: Ross fails to impress his girlfriends father who proves tricky to get on with, and Phoebe makes Chandler and Monica paranoid by chronicling their every move in a supposedly fictional book she is writing.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Pauls the Man
programme.desc: Ross accidentally takes Elizabeth to the same cabin where Elizabeths strict father takes Rachel for a romantic vacation, and Monica panics Chandler by planning their wedding.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Since she is the only one who knows that Chandler plans to ask Monica to marry him, Phoebe helps Chandler shop for a wedding ring, while Rachels wish for Paul to reveal his emotions creates a new set of problems.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandlers romantic marriage proposal is ruined when Monicas former boyfriend returns, Joey accidentally buys a boat, and a dormitory incident reminds Ross how young his girlfriend is.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica is left reeling from Richards declaration that he loves her and wants to marry her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everyone is toasting Chandler and Monicas engagement, but after Chandler fails to come up with the goods in the bedroom, he begins to worry about the consequences. Elsewhere, Monica thinks Rachel is trying to steal her glory when she catches her kissing Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monicas dream of a fairytale wedding fizzles out after she learns what her parents have done with her wedding fund. Meanwhile, Rachel fumes when a teasing Joey stumbles across her juicy late-night reading material.

programme.title: Michael McIntyres The Wheel
programme.desc: Michael McIntyre hosts as Dermot OLeary, Dame Jessica Ennis-Hill, Roman Kemp, Adam Kay, Angela Scanlon, Kay Burley and Alex Brooker try to help the contestants answer their way to a fortune.

programme.title: Con Air
programme.desc: Set aboard a federal aeroplane transporting convicts to a maximum security facility. One passenger, due for release on parole after eight years in prison, is about to discover that his flight home will turn into a disaster when the prisoners hijack the aircraft. In a race against time, he joins forces with the US Marshal to prevent the criminals from blowing the plane and all on board to pieces.

programme.title: Jack Whitehall: Live at the Hammersmith Apollo
programme.desc: From dates at Nandos to lads holidays in Magaluf, British customer service etiquette to having sex at his parents house. Jack dances from one story to another. Recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson thrash out the hot stories of the week and are joined by actor and comedian Johnny Vegas and Joe Lycett.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha, Wayne, Colin, and Ryan are joined by Greg Proops to play Lets Make a Date, Song Styles, Secret, Film Dub, Dubbing, and Scenes From a Hat. Featuring special guest Tony Cavalero.

programme.title: Dara OBriain Talks Funny: Live in London
programme.desc: Recorded at the world famous Hammersmith Apollo before 3,300 fans, this is the culmination of Daras massive 2008 sellout tour of the UK and Ireland. programme.desc: Nat handles a Freedom of Information request.

programme.title: Killer Britain With Dermot Murnaghan
programme.desc: In July 1991, Michael Sams kidnapped Julie Dart and took her to his workshop in Newark where he kept her in a makeshift coffin before killing her after she tried to escape.

programme.title: Nightmare in Suburbia
programme.desc: Despite being spotted in a pub with blood on his hands, the cold-blooded killer of teenager Colette Aram wasnt arrested until 17 years later.

programme.title: Nightmare in Suburbia
programme.desc: In 2000, the grizzly remains of a man were found in Sheffield and, with just a clay mould of his decomposed face, police uncovered a chilling tale of deathly greed.

programme.title: Robbie Coltranes Critical Evidence
programme.desc: In 1984 a parcel bomb was sent to a 53-year-old grandmother living in a tranquil English village. She died of horrific injuries. No one understood why it had happened. The only evidence detectives had was the bomb itself.

programme.title: Robbie Coltranes Critical Evidence
programme.sub-title: DNA of a Murder : Andrew George
programme.desc: Hilda Murrell, a 78-year-old distinguished rose-grower, was found dead in woodland near her home town. Despite a huge investigation by the British police, detectives failed to find her killer and conspiracy theories muddied the waters still further.

programme.title: Murder Detective
programme.desc: Graham Hill is a hugely experienced crime fighter. In his career, he has seen it all and has been a part of some of the most significant murder hunts of recent years.

programme.title: Murder Detective
programme.desc: Maheswaran Kaneshan sought asylum in the UK, but became caught up in a gang feud that turned deadly. His life was controlled by the gang that had brought him to London. Gang bosses brutally beat Maheswaran, causing him to later die in hospital.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: In broad daylight on a busy Woolwich street on 22nd May 2013, serving soldier Fusilier Lee Rigby was attacked and murdered by Islamic extremists Michael Adebolajo and Michael Adebowale.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: A look back at the rape and murder of Clare Wood that led to the launch of Clares Law.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: When a 71-year-old man is found shot dead in a field, investigators suspect his killing is the result of a robbery. But as new evidence surfaces, detectives develop a wild theory to explain his death, and only a voice from the grave can prove it.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: When a Navy officer succumbs to a mysterious illness after months at sea, nothing makes sense, until an autopsy yields a stunning revelation. All roads lead to dead ends, until they zero in on an unlikely target.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: A womans sudden death in a Detroit hotel unravels a dark web of secrets, lies, and deadly coincidences that hint at something far more sinister.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: When Diana Burrow is found unresponsive, her family and police suspect her death is from natural causes until small-town whispers reveal the neighbourhood criminals may be hiding a deadly secret.

programme.title: Cold Case Files: Murder in the Bayou
programme.desc: When the supposed great-niece of Marilyn Monroe, Krystal Jean Baker, 13, is found dead beneath a bridge in Texas Bayou Country, investigators are stumped. The quest to track down her killer will take investigators 14 years, and lead to a new law.

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: When 31-year-old real estate appraiser Mike Williams mysteriously vanishes while duck hunting in the Florida swamps in December of 2000, authorities suspect he accidentally drowned.

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: In 1980, radio intern Helene Pruszynski is found brutalized in a snowy field; after 37 years of fruitless leads, investigators tap into a public genealogy website that leads them across the country to the killer theyve been hunting.

programme.title: Crime in Progress
programme.desc: An unprecedented look inside high-stakes criminal cases, captured through the raw lens of body cam and surveillance footage. During a routine traffic stop in New Mexico, an officers radio falls silent, triggering a frantic manhunt.

programme.title: Tell Me How I Died
programme.desc: After a pastors second wife is killed in a tragic car crash, it raises suspicions over the death of his first wife; her death never sat easy with pathologist Dr. Wayne Ross, who is eager to reopen the older case he never forgot.

programme.title: Cold Case Files: DNA Speaks
programme.desc: When 14-year-old Nacole Smith is brutally murdered in 1995 on a wooded path in Atlanta, the police and community rally around her mother as she seeks justice. It will take decades and a second brutal assault to finally reveal Nacoles killer.

programme.desc: Benjamin Delgadillo: Step inside the criminal underworld as a small robbery spirals into brutal violence and reveals the ruthless code that governs life on the edge. (S2, Ep 6)

programme.title: Killer Britain With Dermot Murnaghan

programme.title: Nightmare in Suburbia

programme.title: Nightmare in Suburbia
programme.desc: In 2000, the grizzly remains of a man were found in Sheffield and, with just a clay mould of his decomposed face, police uncovered a chilling tale of deathly greed.

programme.title: Robbie Coltranes Critical Evidence
programme.desc: In 1984 a parcel bomb was sent to a 53-year-old grandmother living in a tranquil English village. She died of horrific injuries. No one understood why it had happened. The only evidence detectives had was the bomb itself.

programme.title: Robbie Coltranes Critical Evidence

programme.desc: Hilda Murrell, a 78-year-old distinguished rose-grower, was found dead in woodland near her home town. Despite a huge investigation by the British police, detectives failed to find her killer and conspiracy theories muddied the waters still further.

programme.title: Murder Detective
programme.desc: Graham Hill is a hugely experienced crime fighter. In his career, he has seen it all and has been a part of some of the most significant murder hunts of recent years.

programme.title: Murder Detective
programme.desc: Maheswaran Kaneshan sought asylum in the UK, but became caught up in a gang feud that turned deadly. His life was controlled by the gang that had brought him to London. Gang bosses brutally beat Maheswaran, causing him to later die in hospital.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: In broad daylight on a busy Woolwich street on 22nd May 2013, serving soldier Fusilier Lee Rigby was attacked and murdered by Islamic extremists Michael Adebolajo and Michael Adebowale.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: A look back at the rape and murder of Clare Wood that led to the launch of Clares Law.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: When a 71-year-old man is found shot dead in a field, investigators suspect his killing is the result of a robbery. But as new evidence surfaces, detectives develop a wild theory to explain his death, and only a voice from the grave can prove it.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: When a Navy officer succumbs to a mysterious illness after months at sea, nothing makes sense, until an autopsy yields a stunning revelation. All roads lead to dead ends, until they zero in on an unlikely target.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: A womans sudden death in a Detroit hotel unravels a dark web of secrets, lies, and deadly coincidences that hint at something far more sinister.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: When 31-year-old real estate appraiser Mike Williams mysteriously vanishes while duck hunting in the Florida swamps in December of 2000, authorities suspect he accidentally drowned.

programme.title: Cold Case Files

programme.title: Crime in Progress

programme.title: Tell Me How I Died

programme.title: Cold Case Files: DNA Speaks

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Filthy Fortunes
programme.desc: Matt scrambles to clear a bat-infested barn and turn a sea of Santa Clauses into big money. While tackling jobs in Michigan and Massachusetts, Matt finds a lost cache of rare coins.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase Morrill and his team renovate an off-the-grid hunting cabin deep in the woods of Oxford, ME. They have a budget of $20,000 and a time frame of six weeks, but can they get the cabin done before winter comes?

programme.title: Naked and Afraid
programme.sub-title: This Is Gonna Suck So Bad
programme.desc: From Mexicos Yucatán to Botswanas Okavango Delta, fan favourites Ernie and Mandy return -- this time for 21 days. Armed with new tools and improved skills, can they survive? Or will Africa eat them alive?

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Reports of shadowy figures and malevolent spirits from Caribbean folklore draw Phil and Heather to Trinidad. They explore a notorious plantation and an abandoned island once used as a leper colony, searching for the source of the chilling activity.

programme.title: Conspiracies and Cover-Ups
programme.desc: Andrew probes COVID-19s mysterious origin from all angles. Using intelligence tradecraft, expert interviews, and real-world analysis, he investigates natural spillover, lab-related, and accidental release theories.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Dark matter is one of the greatest mysteries of the universe facing scientists today. Could it be the cause of supermassive black holes and mass extinctions?

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Astronomers have discovered bizarre planets very different to Earth out in the galaxy, but will we ever locate a planet similar to our own?

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis
programme.desc: JJ agrees to take on a team from Arizona, but come race night the MSO realises their opponents have an arsenal of ridiculously expensive cars.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis
programme.desc: The streets of Las Vegas havent been kind to the MSO. Local racer Brett tries to take advantage by putting together a group of hand-picked ringers to whoop on JJ and his family.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: A 1979 Jeep CJ-7 Renegade rolls into the workshop, but its rusted out. Ant repairs the damage, while Mike revives its tired faux-denim stonewashed upholstery.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys cosy in for a good old-fashioned Alaskan movie night as they reflect on their most life-changing rescues and self-shot updates from homesteaders.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: In Northern California, the Raneys give a masterclass on rebuilding from scratch when they help a family whose homestead was destroyed in a devastating wildfire.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: After Hurricane Helene obliterates a 100-year-old farmhouse in NC, a once self-sufficient family is left with nothing. The Raneys face their most urgent mission yet: to rebuild a fourth-generation homestead, making it 100% self-sustainable.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Homestead: Safer at Home-stead
programme.desc: The Raney family turns cameras on themselves as they shelter in place on their Alaskan homestead. When a moose kill is discovered dangerously close to their cabin, Marty attempts to track the predators.

programme.title: Moonshiners
programme.desc: Tim, Tickle and Henry confront the rogue shiners who torched their stash house. Mike and Jerry make liquor from candy roaster squash.

programme.title: Vantara: Sanctuary Stories
programme.desc: Forrest Galante reveals Vantaras care of every animal, from 3D beaks and acupuncture for elephants, to a team helping a cub walk again.

programme.title: Moonshiners: True Crime and Shine
programme.desc: When a moonshine kingpin murdered a rogue bootlegger in 1948, fear kept witnesses silent.

programme.title: The Supercar Showroom
programme.desc: Its the dream car dealership for those with dream budgets: a Yorkshire-based supercar salesroom in a unique location, just metres from Leeds East Airport runway.

programme.title: Shortys Dream Shop
programme.sub-title: Ford vs. Shorty: The Texas Torino
programme.desc: Shortys is traditionally a Chevy shop, but this week, a Ford collector whos always dreamed of a Shorty paint job convinces them to take on his 71 Torino GT. But what looks like a pretty straight car is hiding a can of worms underneath.

programme.title: Extreme RVs
programme.sub-title: Newmar King Aire; Forks Continental Coach; EarthRoamer
programme.desc: Extreme RVs features rolling million-dollar mega-mansions with incredible themed designs and luxurious interiors that put most real homes to shame.

programme.title: Extreme RVs
programme.sub-title: Foretravel Quad Slide; American Coach Heritage; Heintz Designs 1957 Airfloat Landyacht
programme.desc: Extreme RVs features rolling million-dollar mega-mansions with incredible themed designs and luxurious interiors that put most real homes to shame.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis
programme.desc: Markelle from Arkansas steps up to the plate with a group of hand-picked racers from all over the country.

programme.title: NASAs Unexplained Files
programme.desc: When NASA begins a strange experiment to communicate with dolphins, experts investigate if its a prelude to making alien contact and some fear that such a discovery could have apocalyptic consequences.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: Josh follows in the footsteps of Indiana Jones as he searches in Israel and Ethiopia for the lost Ark of the Covenant, the sacred relic said to contain the Ten Commandments and possess extraordinary powers.

As Alex pretends to speak fluent Italian, he and Chelsea bond over a banana. Will Alex be honest with Chelsea or will she fall for his charm? Colin, who was born with a birth defect resulting in a missing leg, has been on over 100 first dates.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Loud and proud duo Fay and Wayne kick things off in the Midlands with their comfy country cuisine featuring a beef Wellington main course. Day two sees retired lovebirds Rob and Maz hosting with their take on nouveau cuisine.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Glasgow, tour operator and laird Davey decides to make a confession which leaves his wife Irene and the rest of the guests gobsmacked. Has it scuppered their plans for bagging the grand?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One in a London Nightclub
programme.desc: Dean wants to relive his clubbing days by organising a nostalgic wedding in a London nightclub. His bride-to-be, Sandra, has been made to wait nearly a decade for her big day and shes desperate for her childhood church followed by a reception in a castle.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Adam lets fate take a hand as he plans to give his fiancee Bianca the wedding of a lifetime. Will the pregnant bride-to-be get her elegant, classic day to remember?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Ryan wants his and bride-to-be Marias wedding to take place at the college where they first met. Will Maria give Ryan top marks or will he face detention?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Holly is hoping for a warm, cosy, family wedding, but her cowboy-loving fiance Cole has a Wild West theme in mind.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Khloe is dreaming of a romantic wedding ceremony on a Mediterranean beach, but her fiance Lawrence has his sights set on a funfair-themed day.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Anthony Wedo, president and CEO of buffet restaurant organisation Buffets, Inc., goes undercover posing as a construction worker taking part in a reality TV competition to win enough money to start up his own business.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Amit Kleinberger, CEO and co-owner of frozen yogurt chain Menchies, goes undercover posing as a participant in a reality TV contest who is trying to win money to fund and develop a new business.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon is in Atlanta, Georgia, to visit Michons, a family-run smokehouse restaurant with a future thats about to go up in smoke because of mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michons owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped that one day he would pass his beloved restaurant over to his daughter, Natalie. But when he suffered a collapsed lung, the handover came about much earlier than expected.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Philadelphia to visit a Mexican restaurant called Zocalo. At one point, this restaurant played Cupid to a young couple who met, fell in love and married while working here together. Greg and Mary Russell loved the place so much that they pooled their resources and bought it. However, it seems that Cupids arrow had a poisoned tip: the restaurant has become an albatross around their necks.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, to try to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over the failing Spin-a-Yarn restaurant in 1995 and began to turn it into a success. He fell in love with a waitress, but the restaurant and the marriage are now failing.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Italian bistro Charlies in La Verne, Southern California, which was bought in 2010 by Tatiana Leiva, her mother and her sister. Tatiana didnt actually put any money into the purchase herself, but her hard-working mother and sister did because they felt they were helping to fulfil Tatianas dream. They even put up their houses as collateral! Unfortunately, soon Charlies was in trouble.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Baltimore to try to turn around a restaurant that is suffering because its owner, Denise Whiting, has managed to alienate the surrounding community. When Denise opened Cafe Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of her unwavering business ambition the local community decided to boycott the restaurant, leaving Denise frustrated by falling sales.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Big Zuu take on Danny Dyer and Helen Bauer. Mat Ewins joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Cosmic Jam
programme.desc: In his 1996 debut live stand-up show, Bill Bailey demonstrates the influence that Cockney music has had on the great classical composers, and warns of the perils of a world ruled by insects, while playing the second-biggest gong in Britain.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: The gang are beside themselves with excitement as they have tickets to see Take That in Belfast. When Ma Mary forbids them to go they decide that they will stop at nothing to get there.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: The Quinn family cause uproar at a family wedding, and when the girls, and James fail to behave themselves at the reception the consequences are very serious indeed. Especially for poor cousin Eammon.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Uncut
programme.desc: An extended version of the show in which team captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock join host Jimmy Carr and guests Tess Daly, Stephen Mangan, Kian Egan and Tony Law.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Uncut
programme.desc: An extended version of the show in which team captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock join host Jimmy Carr. With guests David ODoherty, Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Jamelia and Paul Foot.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Big Zuu take on Danny Dyer and Helen Bauer. Mat Ewins joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom Robert has just three weeks to plan his wedding. But will his trusting bride Adrianna be impressed with his royal African theme ceremony?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Matthew has just three weeks, £14,000 and the help of his best man to make his dreams of a Roman Empire-themed wedding come true. But will his fiancee Zoe-Jade approve?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: James and Hollie are desperate to get hitched, but dont have the cash. They are given £12,000 and the chance for James to prove himself to the woman he loves by making every decision about the wedding alone. He and Hollie have agreed in the presence of a lawyer that she will know nothing until the big day. Will James find the perfect venue and get the dress right?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Paul Quentel, president of Alfred Angelo, the second largest bridal retailer in the USA, goes undercover in his own company posing as a personal trainer who is competing in a reality TV show to win money to start his own business.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Mike Bloom, president and COO of discount retail chain Family Dollar, goes undercover posing as a contestant on a reality TV competition who is trying to win money to open his own bar.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Willie and Julie kick off this competition from the Cambridge area with their hearty menu. Day two sees healthy food options from sporty pair Jeavon and Steve, and finally its Italian Ricas turn with the help of Lynne to cook a high-end meal.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cambridgeshire, big kids Wizz and Luke throw a childrens party-themed night, Diana and Mark do fine dining, and Ed and Steve serve an enormous trifle.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cheltenham, perfectionist Michelle and her husband, Bryan Adams tribute singer Russell, are the first to host. Day two is Lydia and Calums turn to host and they hope a rustic theme will bag them the grand.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to South Philadelphia to try to turn around family-run restaurant Chiarellas, which is struggling to survive in an area full of thriving Italian establishments. That isnt the only problem facing owners Tommy and Dina Defino. Their marriage is on the verge of collapse.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay returns to restaurants he tried to help in the past, such as Downcity in Providence, RI, Classic American in West Babylon, NY, and Davide in Boston to check on their progress.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay revisits La Frite, Kingston Cafe, Capri and Spanish Pavilion to see if the owners succeeded in following his advice.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Boston for the first of two visits to La Galleria 33, an Italian restaurant opened in 2006 by raucous sisters Rita and Lisa. The two siblings grew up working at their fathers successful restaurant nearby, but when they opened their own establishment, the customers didnt materialise. The sisters blame everything from the weather to the tablecloths for their failures.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon confronts restaurant owners and sisters Rita and Lisa with an ultimatum. His stand-off with the siblings is just the beginning of the explosive action. He finds himself stuck in a war between the waiters, chefs and owners over who is to blame for the restaurants failings. Can Gordon wrangle these hapless proprietors and stubborn employees back into one productive team and save the restaurant?

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: The normally infallible Alex Horne makes a rare, condiment-based error for which he will no doubt be punished by the seething Taskmaster. And Joanna Page smashes up a sheep.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: A compilation of best bits from recent episodes of `8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown hosted by Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent, featuring Jon Richardson, Joe Wilkinson, Richard Ayoade, Judi Love, Roisin Conaty, Lee Mack, and many more.

programme.title: Sean Lock: Keep It Light
programme.desc: Sean Lock presents his blend of surreal yet insightful stand-up comedy, recorded at the Theatre Royal in Margate, in which he airs his mischievous views on parenting, old age, and immigration.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Jenny Joyce organises a 50s style prom, Erin, Clare and Michelle are looking for prom dates, but Orla has already got someone special in mind. And a new girl arrives.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: As Christmas approaches, excitement in Derry is at fever pitch for President Bill Clintons visit. Meanwhile James gets a surprise and Granda Joe embarks on a mysterious plan of his own.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Uncut
programme.desc: An extended edition of the show with Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, who are joined by celebrity guests Alex Jones, Joe Wilkinson, Danny Dyer and Miles Jupp.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Uncut
programme.desc: With guests Henning Wehn, Denise van Outen, Nick Helm and Vernon Kay.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: A compilation of best bits from recent episodes of `8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown hosted by Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent, featuring Jon Richardson, Joe Wilkinson, Richard Ayoade, Judi Love, Roisin Conaty, Lee Mack, and many more.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire has overdone it in Halloweens past and the neighbours definitely dont share her enthusiasm, so this year shes being forced to tone it down and keep it `kid-friendly.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry and his friends try to reclaim their youth by forming a boy band, but they quickly learn they arent kids anymore; Adam has the keys to Ericas downtown apartment but realises hes not cut out for city life.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.sub-title: The Strangest Affair of All Time
programme.desc: When Murray goes out of town, Jane Bales attempts to make a power grab at the Ottoman Empire, forcing Beverly to retaliate; Adam is thrilled to learn that Dave Kim also plans to attend NYU.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Calvin the over-enthusiastic groom plans a monster Scottish wedding for him and his bride-to-be Leanne at Loch Ness. Will his big idea come back to bite him?

programme.title: LEGO Masters: Australia vs The World
programme.desc: The builders enter the Lego Masters bakery, where they must choose an object to replicate with Lego. Whose delicious dupe will look just as good as the real deal and fool the judges?

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Calamity and comedy go hand-in-hand when the Dunphys throw Luke a birthday bash. The party is certainly memorable, especially when Cam shows up as Fizbo the clown.

programme.title: The Amazing Race Australia: Celebrity Edition
programme.desc: The race takes a spiritual turn in the ancient Cambodian kingdom of Angkor Wat. But which team will tap out when lunch has more legs than expected?!

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: Tensions boil over as Jazz and Inga come under the spotlight once again. Philip gets into hot water with Georgia. Angus is inspired to start dating older women.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Manhattans Black Pearl, a struggling seafood restaurant with three different owners running the kitchen, has come to chef Gordon Ramsay for help; one of the owners is resistant to change and questions every move Gordon makes.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The owners of J. Willys Bar and Grille in South Bend, Indiana, are $1.2 million in debt and contact chef Gordon Ramsay in hope that he can help give them a fresh start; Gordon teaches the inexperienced cooks how to make various dishes.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The groups fractured dynamic looms large at the sixth Commitment Ceremony, and one couple are hanging by a thread.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Harsh truths are laid bare as homestays begin, with family and friends rather unhelpfully wading in to one couples relationship.

programme.title: Rat Race
programme.desc: A Las Vegas casino owner decides to set up a special event for his highest rollers. Lucky tokens in his slot machines entitle six punters to enter a race across America with $2 million as the prize. He makes a killing on the bets placed on the players, while the contestants dash for the cash.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart forges his school report so Homer will let him and Lisa go to Kamp Krusty for the Summer, but the dream camp turns out to be a sweat shop and Bart leads an uprising. Meanwhile, with the kids away, Homer gets into the best shape of his life and love breaks out in the Simpson household.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Marge and the kids take a seaside holiday, Homer is unwittingly tempted by a British femme fatale.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Grampa is accused of a crime; Kent Brockman questions his career.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Odd couple Skinner and Chalmers embark on an 800-mile car ride to Cincinnati to attend an administrators convention.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa calls her teacher, Ms. Hoover, a hack and refuses to apologize; Lisa learns Ms. Hoovers private pain.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Comic book guy and his wife Kumiko debate having a baby; comic book guys origin story is revealed.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart finds his old teachers diary and learns a surprising secret; Lisa discovers an even bigger surprise.

programme.title: Animal Farm

programme.title: Little Women
programme.desc: Following the lives of four sisters, Amy, Jo, Beth and Meg, as they come of age in America in the aftermath of the Civil War. Though all very different from each other, the March sisters stand by each other through difficult and changing times.

programme.title: My Spy
programme.desc: Nine-year-old Sophie catches JJ, a hardened CIA operative, spying on her family during a routine surveillance operation. In exchange for not blowing his cover, JJ begrudgingly agrees to show the precocious girl how to become a spy. What at first seems like an easy task soon turns into a battle of wits as Sophie proves you dont need much experience to outsmart a seasoned agent.

programme.title: Stormbreaker
programme.desc: Following the suspicious death of his uncle, 14-year-old Alex Rider is forcibly recruited into MI6. After a gruelling period of training the schoolboy turned reluctant super spy is sent on his first mission. Will it be his last?

programme.title: The Greatest Showman
programme.desc: P.T. Barnum indulges his limitless imagination to create the Barnum and Bailey circus in the 1800s. Featuring musical numbers, exotic performers and daring feats, the mesmerizing spectacle takes the world by storm to become the greatest show on Earth.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners Movie
programme.desc: Will, Simon, Neil and Jay have now finished sixth form and have decided to celebrate with a holiday in Malia on the island of Crete. They naturally see this as the perfect opportunity to grab as much sun, sea and sex as possible. Unfortunately their insecurities and dire approaches to any woman unfortunate enough to cross their path means it looks as though theyll have to content themselves with drinking instead.

programme.desc: On the last day of school in May 1976, students at a suburban Texas high school wait, lackadaisically, for classes to end.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: The average price of the recipes in this episode is just £1.96 per portion. Jamie cooks up a roast shoulder of lamb with all the trimmings and turns the leftovers into Moroccan-style crispy pastillas.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Here, the average price of the recipes is just £1.89 per portion. Jamie cooks up glorious pot roasted ham hocks with parsley sauce, and roasts a butternut squash which he turns into a stunning squash and spinach rotolo.

programme.title: James Martin Digs Deep
programme.desc: Jamess fruit trees and strawberries bear enough fruit for a delicious pavlova, one of his favourite puddings.

programme.title: James Martin Digs Deep
programme.desc: James uses his three-week-old herb bed to cook two delicious recipes: salmon with sorrel and peas and lemon verbena creme brulee for dessert.

programme.title: James Martin Digs Deep
programme.desc: James heads to Yorkshire for advice on tomatoes and, after getting in a tangle planting them back home, is over the moon when the first baby tomatoes appear and he can cook a tasty mozzarella and tomato dish.

programme.title: James Martin Digs Deep
programme.desc: James cooks two of his grannys recipes with his homegrown gooseberries and carrots. He then takes off on his motorbike for a charity bike run delivering chocolate to a childrens hospital.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Bikers journey continues, bringing the boys cruising the roads along the 220-mile River Severn, a region steeped in history and natural beauty.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Bikers reach Devon and Dorset for the final leg of their tour. In Dorset, they visit a watercress farm and meet a blacksmith, and in Devon, they search for biltong and learn all about the fermentation and distilling of white rum.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Ride Route 66
programme.desc: In small town America, the bikers enjoy some retro roadside spectacles that provide entertainment and intrigue, including the huge fibreglass figures known as `muffler men. Dave and Si then enjoy a championship winning apple pie and learn how Route 66 entered American folklore by connecting small town America from east to west and enabling it to prosper.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: On the second leg of their Route 66 adventure, Dave Myers and Si King set out to discover what lies beneath the legend of this iconic road, a journey which takes them through three states. The bikers also visit a roadside museum described as a love letter to Route 66. The current owner, like her father before her, is passionate about keeping the magic of the fabled road alive.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: James Martins British Adventure
programme.desc: James culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands. He is joined by top Scottish Michelin starred chef Tom Kitchin. Their journey begins with an incredible tour of the castle grounds complete with awe inspiring views. James kicks off the cooking with his venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castles walled garden. Next Tom Kitchin dusts of his wellies and heads in to the woods for a bit of wild mushroom foraging.

programme.title: James Martins British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Tom are making their way to Edinburgh but before they can do that, James makes a detour to Edradour whisky distillery in Pitlochry where he makes a delicious whisky chicken with wild mushroom and mustard sauce. Once theyve reached Edinburgh, Tom shares his local knowledge by visiting one of his favourite suppliers, Welch Fishmongers. He learns the secret to their amazing smoked salmon which James uses for a salmon mousse with cucumber and dill pickle and melba toast.

programme.title: Myths and Monsters

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The Brits Win Every War?
programme.desc: Al meets the Auld Enemy, where bitter sibling rivalry is as strong today as its ever been. Joined by Sanjeev Kohli, Al visits Craufurdland Castle to explore the truth behind famous battles from the Wars of Scottish Independence.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The English Win Every War?
programme.desc: Al is joined by proud Frenchman Fred Sirieix to separate the fact from the fiction of the Napoleonic wars. Together they take a deeper look into the battles of Waterloo and Trafalgar.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The British Win Every War?
programme.desc: Al joins forces with German comedian Henning Wehn to delve deeper into the truth behind some WWII myths. Taking to the skies, they explore whether the Spitfire really was the saviour of Britain that it was cracked up to be.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The British Win Every War?
programme.desc: What did the Romans ever do for us? Well, they certainly put the Brits through their paces back in AD 43. Al is joined by Caesar Bruno Tonioli to look at key battle tactics during the Roman Invasion. From wading through the waves in Roman armour, to seeing how hard it was to invade, to the power struggle the Celts went through to hold onto their identity and just how difficult it is to fire a catapult.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The British Win Every War?
programme.desc: Al faces up to one of the worlds superpowers and the richest nation on Earth to discover how the former British colony of America somehow slipped through its fingers. Joining him to fight for his nations honour is comedian Reginald D Hunter.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The British Win Every War?
programme.desc: Its 865 AD and the Great Heathen Army, the Vikings, have invaded Britain. Al takes up his sword to discover the truth behind how the Vikings were so successful in Britain. With John Thomson, they explore the truth of what Vikings really looked like.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The Brits Win Every War?
programme.desc: Al meets the Auld Enemy, where bitter sibling rivalry is as strong today as its ever been. Joined by Sanjeev Kohli, Al visits Craufurdland Castle to explore the truth behind famous battles from the Wars of Scottish Independence.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al Murray is joined by legendary master of stand-up, Anuvab Pal, to explore the truth about Britains rocky relationship with India.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al Murray pairs up with local comedians to explore the history of British colonial rule and its legacy. They find out about the origins of British rule, its impact and how its legacy continues to influence governance, culture, and attitudes.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al and comedian Loyiso Gola embark on a road trip through South Africa to explore the UKs imperial history in the country, from colonial wars to the deals that eventually led to the development of apartheid.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al and comic Kevin Kropinyeri reveal that Captain James Cook was not really a captain, explore the Aboriginal history of Australian cricket, and uncover the often brutal land grabs that impacted Indigenous Australians.
